techniques.evidence_quote: they executed two standard reconnaissance commands against the AWS Simple Email Solution (SES) and Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3): GetSendQuota, ListBuckets
GetBucketVersioning ... GetBucketReplication ... GetBucketObjectLockConfiguration ... GetBucketLogging ... GetBucketRequestPayment ... GetAccelerateConfiguration
the attackers most likely stole solely 2 GB of data, compared to the over 1 TB of data available on the bucket
the attacker utilized the command DeleteBucket to erase all buckets
Send us 0.2 Bitcoin (BTC) to our Bitcoin addresses
left a ransom note ... To recover your lost files and avoid leaking it
an attacker had deleted several AWS S3 buckets

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained valid AWS credentials (root cause unknown) and authenticated to the victim's AWS account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We traced the initial actions of the attacker back to January 18, 2024

evidence_chunks.quote: We traced the initial actions of the attacker back to January 18, 2024
We traced the initial actions of the attacker back to January 18, 2024

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated SES quota and S3 buckets using GetSendQuota and ListBuckets API calls.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they executed two standard reconnaissance commands against the AWS Simple Email Solution (SES) and Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3): GetSendQuota, ListBuckets

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated bucket configuration (versioning, replication, object lock, logging, request payment, accelerate configuration) prior to deletion.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Before the deletion of the buckets, the following commands were recorded in the CloudTrail logs: GetBucketVersioning ... GetBucketReplication ... GetBucketObjectLockConfiguration ...

attack_chain.description: Attacker downloaded approximately 2 GB of data from the S3 buckets prior to deletion.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attackers most likely stole solely 2 GB of data, compared to the over 1 TB of data available on the bucket

evidence_chunks.quote: the attackers most likely stole solely 2 GB of data, compared to the over 1 TB of data available on the bucket
the attackers most likely stole solely 2 GB of data, compared to the over 1 TB of data available on the bucket

attack_chain.description: Attacker issued DeleteBucket API calls to erase all S3 buckets (which had no versioning enabled).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: On February 5th 2024, the attacker utilized the command DeleteBucket to erase all buckets

attack_chain.description: Attacker left a ransom note demanding 0.2 BTC and a fake 'recovery' binary (red herring) to coerce payment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Send us 0.2 Bitcoin (BTC) to our Bitcoin addresses ... Your files are downloaded and backed up on our servers.

initial_access_vector: Unknown — attacker possessed valid AWS credentials (access key) enabling authenticated API actions against SES and S3.
